coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
come: verb. To move nearer to the point of perspective.
commie: noun. A communist; a person with communist sympathies; a supposed communist infiltrator.
con: verb. To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
conn: noun. The duty of directing a ship, usually used with the verb to have or to take and accompanied by the article "the."
emic: adjective. Of or pertaining to the analysis of a cultural system or its features from the perspective of a participant in that culture.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
income: noun. Money one earns by working or by capitalising on the work of others.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
mem: noun. The thirteenth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
memo: noun. A short note; a memorandum.
mien: noun. Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.
mime: noun. A form of acting without words; pantomime.
mimeo: noun. Clipping of mimeograph (“type of copying machine”).
mince: noun. Finely chopped meat; minced meat.
mine: pronoun. That or those belonging to me.
mnemonic: adjective. Especially of a series of ideas, letters, words, etc.: intended to help in remembering.
mom: noun. Mother. used as a term of address for one's wife
mome: noun. A dull or stupid person.
mon: noun. The former currency of Japan until 1870, before the yen.
neon: noun. The chemical element (symbol Ne) with an atomic number of 10.
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
nome: noun. A prefecture or unit of regional government in Greece.
nomen: noun. The family name of an Ancient Roman, designating their gens.
nonce: noun. The one or single occasion; the present reason or purpose.
none: pronoun. Not any of a given number or group.

once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ceo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of CEO.
eco: adjective. Clipping of ecological: environmentally friendly or sensitive.
emo: noun. Any form of guitar - driven alternative rock that is particularly or notably emotional; a broadly encompassing genre of rock music
imo: prepositional phrase. Alternative form of IMO.
meconin: noun. A metabolite of meconinic acid, present in opium.
mei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Midob.
mico: noun. A small South American monkey (Mico melanurus, syn.
mino: noun. A traditional Japanese raincoat made from straw.
mnemon: noun. In Ancient Greek society, a person holding the office of recorder or archivist.
moe: noun. (Can we verify this sense?) Strong interest in, and especially fetishistic attraction towards, fictional characters in anime, manga,
monic: adjective. Of a polynomial whose leading coefficient is one.
nco: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sibe.
nemo: adjective. Acronym of not emanating from main office, i.e. broadcast from some remote location instead.
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
nomic: adjective. Customary; ordinary; applied to the usual spelling of a language, in distinction from strictly phonetic methods.
nonic: adjective. Describing a polynomial that has 9 as the highest exponent of its terms.
oenin: noun. An anthocyanin, the 3- glucoside of malvidin and the red pigment in red wine.
oie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okolie.
omni: noun. A person or animal who is not vegan; one with no particular dietary restrictions.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
